prompt: I already have two rough ideas for adding description-optimization support to a skill: one is a standalone `scripts/optimize_description.py`, the other is to fold it into `skill-creator` as another workflow step. I want you to run an architecture contest over those ideas and anything better you see, then recommend whether we should keep them separate, merge them, or use a hybrid.
assumptions: No interactive clarification was possible during this benchmark run, so requirements were inferred from the prompt and repository context.
The goal is architectural recommendation only; no repository source changes should be proposed as already implemented.
Description optimization must remain usable in both guided skill-authoring sessions and non-interactive/scripted contexts.
Headless/shared-environment support matters because the current documented flow relies on browser and temporary-file behavior.
